questions.q: In which Indian city is the Jantar Mantar observatory located?
questions.explanation: The Jantar Mantar described here is the large astronomical observatory in Jaipur, Rajasthan.

questions.q: What kind of structure is the Jantar Mantar?
questions.explanation: The Jantar Mantar is an observatory made up of large masonry instruments for measuring time and tracking celestial bodies.

questions.q: Who built the Jantar Mantar in Jaipur?
questions.explanation: The Jaipur observatory was built by Maharaja Sawai Jai Singh II, the founder of Jaipur.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Jantar Mantar of Jaipur located?
questions.explanation: Jaipur, home of the observatory, is the capital city of the state of Rajasthan.

questions.q: The Jantar Mantar in Jaipur is famous for housing the world's largest example of which instrument?
questions.explanation: The Samrat Yantra at Jaipur is the world's largest stone sundial.

questions.q: What were the Jantar Mantar instruments primarily used to study?
questions.explanation: The instruments were designed to observe the positions of celestial bodies, predict eclipses, and tell time.

questions.q: From which century does the Jaipur Jantar Mantar date?
questions.explanation: The observatory was completed in the early 18th century, around 1734.

questions.q: What does the name 'Jantar Mantar' broadly refer to?
questions.explanation: 'Jantar Mantar' derives from terms meaning instruments and calculation/formulae, fitting its role as a calculating observatory.

questions.q: Which international body has recognized the Jaipur Jantar Mantar as a World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: UNESCO inscribed the Jaipur Jantar Mantar on its World Heritage List in 2010.

questions.q: Jantar Mantar's giant instruments are mainly constructed from which type of material?
questions.explanation: Unlike small brass instruments, these were built as large fixed masonry structures of stone and plaster.

questions.q: The founder of the Jaipur observatory built how many Jantar Mantar observatories across northern India?
questions.explanation: Sawai Jai Singh II built five Jantar Mantar observatories, at Jaipur, Delhi, Ujjain, Mathura, and Varanasi.

questions.q: What is the name of the world's largest stone sundial at the Jaipur Jantar Mantar?
questions.explanation: The Samrat Yantra (Supreme Instrument) is the giant equinoctial sundial and the largest of its kind in the world.

questions.q: Around what height does the gnomon (the great triangular wall) of the Samrat Yantra rise?
questions.explanation: The Samrat Yantra's triangular gnomon stands roughly 27 metres (about 88 feet) tall, making it a towering structure.

questions.q: The Samrat Yantra sundial can measure time with an accuracy of about which interval?
questions.explanation: Despite being made of stone, the Samrat Yantra can indicate local time to an accuracy of roughly two seconds.

questions.q: Which Jantar Mantar instrument consists of bowl-shaped sundials with markings on concave hemispheres?
questions.explanation: The Jai Prakash Yantra uses hollowed hemispherical bowls with coordinate markings to map the position of the sun.

questions.q: Which set of twelve instruments at the Jaipur observatory is dedicated to the signs of the zodiac?
questions.explanation: The Rashivalaya Yantra comprises twelve instruments, one for each zodiac sign, used for astrological observations.

questions.q: Sawai Jai Singh II, the builder, was a ruler of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh II was the Kachhwaha Rajput ruler of Amber, who founded and ruled the new capital of Jaipur.

questions.q: Under which Mughal emperor's reign did Sawai Jai Singh II begin his observatory projects?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh II undertook his astronomical work during the reign of the Mughal emperor Muhammad Shah.

questions.q: The Jaipur Jantar Mantar is located close to which other famous Jaipur royal landmark?
questions.explanation: The observatory sits adjacent to the City Palace complex in the heart of old Jaipur.

questions.q: Which instrument at the observatory is used to determine the altitude of celestial objects?
questions.explanation: The Unnatamsha Yantra, a large metal ring instrument, is used to measure the altitude of the sun, moon, and stars.

questions.q: The Jaipur Jantar Mantar is the largest and best preserved of Jai Singh's observatories. What is its astronomical tradition primarily based on?
questions.explanation: The instruments were designed for precise naked-eye observation of celestial positions, not telescopic viewing.

questions.q: One purpose the instruments served was the compilation of astronomical tables. What were such tables historically called?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh used the observations to prepare astronomical tables, of the type known in the Islamic tradition as a zij or ephemeris.

questions.q: The Jaipur Jantar Mantar was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in which year?
questions.explanation: UNESCO added the Jaipur Jantar Mantar to the World Heritage List in 2010.

questions.q: Construction of the Jaipur observatory was largely completed by approximately which year?
questions.explanation: The Jaipur Jantar Mantar was built between roughly 1724 and 1734 under Sawai Jai Singh II.

questions.q: How many major astronomical instruments are generally recognized at the Jaipur Jantar Mantar?
questions.explanation: The Jaipur observatory contains around nineteen principal architectural and astronomical instruments.

questions.q: Which pair of cylindrical instruments at Jaipur is used to find the altitude and azimuth, and was useful for observations after sunset?
questions.explanation: The two Ram Yantra structures are open cylindrical instruments used to measure the altitude and azimuth of celestial objects.

questions.q: Jai Singh II reportedly sent scholarly missions abroad and studied which European astronomer's tables before building his observatories?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh consulted European astronomical tables, including those of Philippe de La Hire, in his quest for accurate data.

questions.q: Besides Jaipur, Jai Singh II built another Jantar Mantar observatory in which city, where the Prime Minister has also been known to address rallies nearby?
questions.explanation: Jai Singh II also built a Jantar Mantar in Delhi; its giant masonry instruments stand near a square long used for public gatherings and protests.

questions.q: Which instrument at Jaipur is aligned with the celestial axis to indicate sidereal time and the declination of celestial bodies?
questions.explanation: The Nadivalaya Yantra has two circular dials parallel to the equatorial plane, indicating time and the declination of the sun.

questions.q: Under UNESCO criteria, the Jaipur Jantar Mantar is celebrated chiefly as an outstanding example of what?
options: A monumental astronomical observatory of its period
questions.explanation: UNESCO recognizes it as the most significant, comprehensive, and best-preserved monumental observatory of the early modern period.
